Job Description: Financial Data Visualization Specialist Position Overview: We are seeking a highly skilled and detail-oriented Financial Data Visualization Specialist to create visually appealing and insightful dashboards, similar to the one provided, that showcase key financial metrics, trends, and performance indicators. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in financial data analysis, advanced proficiency in data visualization tools, and the ability to present complex data in a clear and actionable format for stakeholders. This dashboard needs to be able to be used for multiple clients Responsibilities: 1. Dashboard Creation: • Design, develop, and maintain financial dashboards that effectively communicate key metrics such as MRR, revenue, costs, profits, churn rates, and cash balances. • Utilize charts, graphs, and interactive visualizations to highlight insights and trends. 2. Data Analysis: • Extract, clean, and organize financial data from various sources to ensure accuracy and consistency. • Analyze data to identify trends, variances, and actionable insights for management. 3. Collaboration: • Work closely with finance, operations, and leadership teams to understand reporting requirements and business objectives. • Translate financial concepts into meaningful visualizations that non-financial stakeholders can understand. 4. Automation and Integration: • Set up automated data pipelines to ensure dashboards are updated in real-time or on a regular schedule. • Integrate data from various tools such as QuickBooks and Xero . 5. Quality Assurance: • Perform rigorous testing to validate the accuracy and reliability of dashboards and reports. • Regularly review and update dashboards to reflect changes in business priorities or data sources. 6. Presentation: • Present dashboards and findings to stakeholders during meetings or through written reports. • Provide recommendations based on data insights to drive business decisions. Qualifications: • Proven experience in financial data analysis and visualization. • Proficiency in tools for visualization platforms. • Advanced Excel skills (pivot tables, formulas, charts) and familiarity with Google Sheets. • Experience with financial software such as QuickBooks, Xero, or similar tools. • Strong understanding of financial metrics, including MRR, churn rate, operating costs, and revenue streams. • Excellent communication and presentation skills. • Detail-oriented with strong problem-solving abilities. Preferred Skills: • Familiarity with accounting principles and financial forecasting. • Ability to create white-labeled dashboards tailored to guidelines. How to Apply: Submit previous work dashboards in order to be a candidate

Q: Describe a challenge you faced and how you overcame it. Situation: During my internship at Pakistan State Oil, I was tasked with analyzing a vast dataset of over 270,000 rows related to DIGICASH customer information. However, I quickly realized that the dataset was incomplete, with numerous missing values, making it unfit for analysis. Task: My primary responsibility was to make the dataset usable by addressing the missing values and preparing it for meaningful analysis. This included creating a dashboard to provide actionable insights for the organization. Action: I adopted a proactive approach to resolve the issue. I consistently followed up with the IT department to obtain the necessary data and, in parallel, applied logical methods to infer missing values. For example, I deduced missing CNIC values by analyzing patterns such as the last digit. Additionally, I used advanced Excel techniques and Power BI to clean and organize the data, ensuring it was ready for analysis. Result: My efforts resulted in a fully functional, actionable dashboard that provided insights into customer segmentation, regional sales, and behavioral trends. This dashboard not only supported strategic decision-making but also facilitated the re-engagement of over 120 dormant DIGICASH accounts, positively impacting customer retention.
